title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with opening act The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The Swedish band delivered a high-energy show with both new songs and classics like 'Hate To Say I Told You So.'
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist engaged the audience with dynamic stage presence and witty banter.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, of Braintree, will run her third Boston Marathon with the New England Patriots Foundation.
McHugh was diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023 but returned to running after extensive treatment and recovery.
She runs in memory of her friend Susan Hurley and to demonstrate that cancer does not define her life.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey questions Meta's plans to integrate facial recognition into smart glasses, citing privacy concerns.
Markey, Wyden, and Merkley urge Meta to detail how it will protect biometric data and respect privacy rights.
The senators set an April 6 deadline for Meta to address potential misuse of facial recognition technology.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million bond for PFAS-free firefighting gear.
The current gear contains PFAS, chemicals associated with cancer, prompting a call for replacements.
Councilors have concerns over cost and long-term durability, pushing for more review time.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce long-term costs.
The zoo faces $57 million in repairs and $900,000 yearly operational costs, amid declining attendance and revenue.
Transition would focus on native wildlife conservation, with limited access beginning September, official decision pending.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a last-minute microwedding in Somerville during a blizzard on Jan. 26, 2026.
Meredith Goldstein explores the use of 'partner' versus 'boyfriend' in relationships and societal communication changes since the 1990s.
Yowei Shaw discusses cultural embarrassment surrounding male partners on the Love Letters podcast.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback from Burlington will run the Boston Marathon to honor his late father and his own cancer survival.
His father died from glioblastoma in 2020, and Averback survived testicular cancer diagnosed in 2021.
Accepted by Team MGH, Averback overcame a serious blood infection to resume marathon training.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The New England Patriots have shown interest in trading for Philadelphia Eagles' WR A.J. Brown.
Patriots' new safety Kevin Byard is working out with A.J. Brown, fueling trade speculation.
Philadelphia may trade Brown after June 1 to save salary cap space, seeking first-round draft pick.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ended his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Bruins are considering signing Hagens, a 2025 seventh draft pick, to an entry-level contract.
Hagens could provide offensive depth for the Bruins' lineup during the final stretch of the season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' follows Tommy Shelby in 1940 Birmingham, now streaming on Netflix.
'Is This Thing On?' stars Will Arnett, directed by Bradley Cooper, streaming on Disney+.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is now available on HBO Max.
